Kung Fu Panda 2 (2011)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $165,249,063Details
International Box Office $499,588,484
Worldwide Box Office $664,837,547
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $37,748,339
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $20,966,797
Total $58,715,136
Further financial details...

Synopsis

In Kung Fu Panda 2, Po is now living his dream as The Dragon Warrior, protecting the Valley of Peace alongside his friends and fellow kung fu masters, The Furious Five. But Po's new life of awesomeness is threatened by the emergence of a formidable villain, who plans to use a secret, unstoppable weapon to conquer China and destroy kung fu. Po must look to his past and uncover the secrets of his mysterious origins; only then will he be able to unlock the strength he needs to succeed

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$47,656,302 (28.8% of total gross)
Legs:3.47 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:24.9% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$150,000,000 (worldwide box office is 4.4 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,925 opening theaters/3,952 max. theaters, 5.7 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $249,228,089

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: May 26th, 2011 (Wide) by Paramount Pictures
May 26th, 2011 (IMAX) by Paramount Pictures
Video Release: December 13th, 2011 by Dreamworks Animated Video
MPA Rating: PG for sequences of martial arts action and mild violence.
(Rating bulletin 2163, 3/16/2011)
Running Time: 90 minutes
Franchise: Kung Fu Panda
Cast, crew, or production detail: Animal Lead, Epilogue, Prologue, Voiceover/Narration
Film format: 3-D, 3-D - Shot in 3-D, IMAX: DMR
Plot point: Martial Arts, Psychics, Revenge, Talking Animals
Subgenre: Family Adventure
Source:Original Screenplay
Genre:Adventure
Production Method:Digital Animation
Creative Type:Kids Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: DreamWorks Animation
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

In Kung Fu Panda 2, Po is now living his dream as The Dragon Warrior, protecting the Valley of Peace alongside his friends and fellow kung fu masters, The Furious Five. But Po's new life of awesomeness is threatened by the emergence of a formidable villain, who plans to use a secret, unstoppable weapon to conquer China and destroy kung fu. Po must look to his past and uncover the secrets of his mysterious origins; only then will he be able to unlock the strength he needs to succeed
